name: Trial watch pruning pilot

scope.question: How should old no-result trials be removed from active result anticipation without losing later result discovery?

summary: Added explicit trial watch-state support and piloted it on the current registry-linked trial inventory. The policy keeps active result anticipation separate from late no-results and retired no-results archive records. The generated audit now reports active watch, late no-results, retirement candidates, status-repair candidates, and records with captured results despite absent registry-posted results. The pilot moved ten old no-result records out of active anticipation as late_no_results, marked three published D+Q records as results_captured, and found two retirement candidates that still need a recorded final retirement sweep before archive.

materiality_decision.rationale: The work changes trial-watch presentation and maintenance workflow but does not add external evidence, revise efficacy interpretation, or create public activity items.

search_log.summary: Generated the local trial watch report after adding watch-state classification. The report found 47 active result-watch records, 10 late no-results records, 2 retirement candidates, 0 retired archive records, 11 status-repair candidates, and 5 late no-results records missing structured completion dates.

Reviewed existing trial_details, registry_last_checked dates, completion dates, expected-results text, and source IDs. No external result sweep was performed in this policy pass, so no record was marked retired_no_results.

notes: Retirement is intentionally a stricter state than late_no_results. It requires a recorded final sweep before setting retired_no_results.
The active /trials default should no longer include late_no_results or retired_no_results records.
The pilot exposed a data-quality queue: five late no-results records have old completion windows in prose but no structured completion date.
Records with published PMID evidence can be results_captured even when the registry itself has no posted results.
next_actions: Run retirement sweeps for cycloastragenol-retinal-amyloid-registry and libella-aav-htert-aging-phase1-registry before deciding whether to set retired_no_results.
Repair structured completion dates for late no-results records whose completion windows currently live only in expected_results_window prose.
During future scoped surveillance, use the retirement-candidate and status-repair sections of extra/trial-watch-report.md before treating a no-result registry as active anticipation.
